pages.full_text:
Tuesday we went on a short walk in __[?Middlewood]-[?Postock]__ Green & admired the 1902 cricket [?Pavillion]. Then we set off for home. More rain now.

__Sunday July 25th__
We left home & returned for fan belt & bottle tops for the Master. Peter is bringing the Master to Thorpe & John is bringing his van. We dropped Tinder off at Kennels.

We slept in the van, leaving the bed up all week & eating in The Green House. / the Headlands. When we first arrived we didn't dare go into the house for a day & night because W.B's friend, Dr. Tom, could not distinguish between Covid & a hangover. Seamark allowed no one into the house – Ester was breathfeeding & had not had a vaccination. Jane was at the house celebrating her 90 years. Unfortunately she had 2 falls & after the second one her hip was too painful to walk & she went home early in a taxi. V Brave & smiling, but a sad situation. (Wanda has broken her collar bone)
Thomas & Naomi had to swap half way through the week cos of Austin. But a super Thorpe – no rows.
pages.notes: First line location is unclear — appears to read '[?Middlewood] [?Postock] Green' or similar; the crossed-out word before 'Green' is partially legible. The word 'Pavillion' may be 'Pavilion'. 'Breathfeeding' is likely 'breastfeeding' — transcribed verbatim as written. 'Ester' may be a name (Esther). Covid reference helps place this page circa 2020–2021 though volume is labelled 2003–2019; may extend beyond label range. No handwritten page number visible on this side. The heading 'Sunday July 25th' is underlined. 'Middlewood Postock' could also be 'Middlewood, Lostock' — the place name before 'Green' is partially obscured by a strikethrough.

pages.full_text:
On the way home we stopped for High Mass with John [?Warburston] at Eye church. Then on to Kennels & home.

__August 7th Saturday__
Simon & Eve leave for Imogen Maxwell's marriage to Nick Greasley. We are staying at a £32 B&B organised by Conta. Awful reviews. Rich [?foot Ballet] country in Cheshire. Down the road from Jodrell Bank.
    Chatted to Wanda. Lovely "wedding" service at Nick Greasley's home ([?Goosefield]). Speeches v. good. David ill with cancer. Dancing feasting dancing. Cold.
Simon gave lifts back to Thomas, Ed, Frankie & Holly. Wanda has a broken collar bone.
In the morning Miles cooked a bbq. breakfast in the rain, provided by Louisa, for lots of cousins.
A super wedding. Off to [?Th...] [illegible]
photo_captions: Jodrell Bank telescope (photo, no caption text)
pages.notes: Handwritten page number 52 visible in top-right corner. 'John Warburston' could be 'Warburton' or 'Warbeston' — place/surname unclear. '[?foot Ballet]' is unclear — could be 'football' country. '[?Goosefield]' is the approximate reading of Nick Greasley's home venue. Photo glued in lower-right shows what appears to be the Jodrell Bank radio telescope, consistent with the text mentioning Jodrell Bank in Cheshire. The bottom of the page is cut off and the last line is only partially visible. The 'August 7th Saturday' heading is underlined. Covid mention on the left page and the context (Imogen Maxwell's wedding, Wanda's broken collar bone cross-referenced from left page) suggests these pages are from around 2021, beyond the nominal volume range of 2003–2019.
